# Band vs Modality Proportion Analysis
**Generated by:** `scripts/exploration/data_analysis/` — PySpark analysis scripts run against T2 pipeline output on sample Parquet shards

This report documents the proportion of different modalities within each curricular band (B0-B5).

## Distribution Table

| Band | agentic_traces | code | general_text | math | reasoning | research_papers | technical_text |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **B0** | 0.0% | 0.0% | 100.0% | 0.0% | 0.0% | 0.0% | 0.0% |
| **B1** | 0.0% | 0.0% | 100.0% | 0.0% | 0.0% | 0.0% | 0.0% |
| **B2** | 0.0% | 0.0% | 100.0% | 0.0% | 0.0% | 0.0% | 0.0% |
| **B3** | 0.0% | 97.6% | 0.0% | 1.7% | 0.3% | 0.0% | 0.4% |
| **B4** | 0.0% | 1.6% | 0.0% | 0.0% | 0.0% | 98.4% | 0.0% |
| **B5** | 4.7% | 83.6% | 0.0% | 1.2% | 0.0% | 10.3% | 0.1% |

## Key Observations

- **Foundational Clusters (B0, B1, B2)**: These bands are composed entirely (100%) of `general_text`. This indicates that the curriculum starts with basic linguistic understanding.
- **Intermediate Stability (B3)**: Primarily driven by `code` (97.6%), with minor contributions from `math` (1.7%).
- **Advanced Specialization (B4)**: Highly specialized towards `research_papers` (98.4%).
- **Expert Complexity (B5)**: Heavily focused on `code` (83.6%) but introduces a high concentration of `research_papers` (10.3%) and unique `agentic_traces` (4.7%), representing the peak of complexity in the current curriculum.

## Methodology

The analysis was performed using [analyze_band_vs_modality.py](file:///d:/ERA_CAPSTONE/LLM/experiments/2_curirculum_architects/scripts/analyze_band_vs_modality.py) on approximately 334,000 samples from the metadata.
